name: Cologne and Sarrant Loop
description: Cycle 11.6 miles (18.7 km) from Cologne to Sarrant through France's scenic Gers region, connecting two charming medieval villages.
region_intro: Touring cycling around Cologne offers diverse landscapes, from the flat, scenic paths along the Rhine River to the varied terrain of its urban green spaces and nearby natural parks. The region is characterized by the wide floodplains of the Rhine, providing consistently flat routes, and extensive green belts like the Stadtwald and Grüngürtel with winding trails and serene ponds. Further afield, areas like the Rhineland Nature Park and Königsforst present more varied scenery, including forests and hills, suitable for different cycling preferences. Best touring cycling routes around Cologne The most popular touring cycling route is From Cologne to Sarrant, a 11.6 miles (18.7 km) trail that takes 1 hour 10 minutes to complete. This moderate route features a steady elevation gain. Another top favourite among local touring cyclists is From Sarrant to the sculptures of Homps, a moderate 10.8 miles (17.3 km) path. This route offers a mix of terrain with some elevation changes. Local touring cyclists also love the Cologne – Thoux Saint-Cricq Lake loop from Cologne, a 17.3 miles (27.8 km) trail leading through varied landscapes, often completed in about 2 hours 12 minutes. Touring cycling around Cologne is defined by the Rhine River, extensive urban green spaces, and nearby forests. The network offers options for different ability levels, from easy riverside paths to more challenging routes in natural parks. The routes in Cologne are highly rated by the komoot community with an average score of 4.1 stars from over 14 reviews. More than 124 touring cyclists have used komoot to explore Cologne's varied terrain.
region_faq:
Q: How many touring cycling routes are available around Cologne?
A: There are over 50 touring cycling routes around Cologne, catering to various preferences. You'll find a good mix, with 37 routes considered moderate, 11 more difficult, and 3 easy options for a relaxed ride.

Q: What kind of terrain can I expect on touring cycling routes near Cologne?
A: Cologne offers diverse terrain for touring cyclists. You can enjoy consistently flat paths along the Rhine River, explore winding trails and serene ponds in urban green spaces like the Stadtwald and Grüngürtel, or venture into more varied scenery with forests and hills in areas like the Rhineland Nature Park and Königsforst.

Q: What is the best time of year for touring cycling in Cologne?
A: The best time for touring cycling in Cologne is generally from spring through autumn. The weather is usually pleasant, making it ideal for exploring the riverside paths, urban parks, and nearby natural areas. Summer offers longer daylight hours, while spring and autumn provide comfortable temperatures and beautiful scenery.

Q: Are there easy touring cycling routes suitable for beginners in Cologne?
A: Yes, there are easy touring cycling routes perfect for beginners. The flat paths along the Rhine River are particularly suitable for relaxed rides. For example, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e931159974/from-cologne-to-sarrant" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;From Cologne to Sarrant&lt;/a&gt; route is a moderate option that can be enjoyed by those looking for a steady ride.

Q: Are there any circular touring cycling routes around Cologne?
A: Yes, many touring cycling routes around Cologne are designed as loops, allowing you to start and finish in the same location. An example is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/37584353" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Cologne – Thoux Saint-Cricq Lake loop from Cologne&lt;/a&gt;, which takes you through varied landscapes and brings you back to your starting point.

Q: Where can I find more challenging touring cycling routes near Cologne?
A: For more challenging touring cycling routes, consider exploring areas like the Königsforst or the Rhineland Nature Park. These regions offer varied scenery with forests and hills, providing a greater effort for experienced cyclists. While the routes listed here are moderate, the region has 11 routes classified as difficult.

Q: Are there family-friendly touring cycling routes in Cologne?
A: Cologne offers several family-friendly cycling options, especially within its urban green spaces and along the flat sections of the Rhine. Parks like the Rheinpark, Stadtwald, and Grüngürtel provide safe and scenic paths suitable for all ages. The Stadtwald, for instance, features winding trails and expansive meadows, perfect for a family outing. For more information on parks and gardens, you can visit &lt;a href="https://www.cologne-tourism.com/arts-culture/sights/parks-gardens" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;cologne-tourism.com&lt;/a&gt;.

Q: Can I bring my dog on touring cycling trails around Cologne?
A: Many of Cologne's outdoor areas, including the Green Belt and parts of the Stadtwald, are dog-friendly, making them suitable for cycling with your canine companion. Always ensure your dog is on a leash where required and be mindful of other trail users. It's advisable to check specific park regulations before your visit.

Q: What scenic viewpoints or landmarks can I see while cycling around Cologne?
A: Cycling around Cologne offers numerous scenic views and landmarks. Along the Rhine, you'll get spectacular views of the Cologne Cathedral and the Old Town. Routes often pass through charming areas like the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/3908053" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;city of Cologne&lt;/a&gt; itself, or historic sites like the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/3770567" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Medieval village of Sarrant&lt;/a&gt;. The Rheinpark also offers beautiful city skyline views.

Q: Are there any interesting attractions along the touring cycling routes?
A: Yes, several interesting attractions can be found along or near the touring cycling routes. You might encounter historical sites such as the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/1437059" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Bastide and Cologne hall&lt;/a&gt;, or the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/6381346" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Cadours Market Hall&lt;/a&gt;. Natural viewpoints like the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/3825461" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Garac Viewpoint&lt;/a&gt; also offer picturesque stops.

Q: What do other touring cyclists enjoy the most about touring cycling in Cologne?
A: The touring cycling routes around Cologne are highly rated by the komoot community, with an average score of 4.1 stars from over 14 reviews. Cyclists often praise the diverse landscapes, from the flat, scenic paths along the Rhine to the varied terrain of its urban green spaces and nearby natural parks, offering something for every preference.

Q: Are there options for public transport or parking near touring cycling routes in Cologne?
A: Cologne has a well-developed public transport network, making many starting points for cycling routes easily accessible. For routes within the city's green spaces like the Grüngürtel or Stadtwald, public transport connections are generally good. Parking facilities are also available at various entry points to larger parks and nature areas, though it's always advisable to check specific locations beforehand.
tips:
Cologne in the Gers is not known for its water but for its beautiful medieval square and its beautiful hall.
Cologne was built on the plan of royal lowland bastides: the city stretched around two perpendicular axes. The city is made up of square or perpendicular blocks around the central square and its hall.
The hall surrounds a building with belfry and bell
The square is superb with its cover and its half-timbered houses.
A vestige of moats is also present.

Medieval village classified among the "most beautiful villages of France". You enter the village through a very beautiful arched doorway to discover small streets lined with tall, colorful stone and half-timbered houses built around the Saint-Vincent church. Do not miss the bookstore which is a nugget and which serves delicious sandwiches!

faqs.question: What is the difficulty level of this cycling route?
faqs.answer: The route is rated as moderate. It covers approximately 18.7 kilometers with an elevation gain of about 174 meters. While mostly paved and suitable for various touring bicycles, it includes some inclines that require a good level of fitness. For example, one segment has an average gradient of 3.5% over 1.1 km.

faqs.question: How long does it typically take to complete this cycling route?
faqs.answer: On average, cyclists can expect to complete this route in approximately 1 hour and 10 minutes, depending on their pace and stops.

faqs.question: Is this route suitable for beginner cyclists?
faqs.answer: While rated moderate, the route is generally accessible. However, the presence of some inclines means that absolute beginners might find it a bit challenging. Cyclists with a good level of fitness will find it enjoyable and manageable.

faqs.question: What kind of scenery can I expect along the way?
faqs.answer: The route offers picturesque views of the Gers region's traditional Gascon landscapes. You'll cycle through rolling green hills, agricultural fields, and charming villages. Both the starting point, Cologne, and the destination, the Medieval village of Sarrant, are notable for their historic architecture and charm.

faqs.question: Where can I park to access this cycling route?
faqs.answer: As the route connects two villages, you can typically find parking in either Cologne or the Medieval village of Sarrant. Look for public parking areas within the villages to begin your ride.

faqs.question: What is the best time of year to cycle this route?
faqs.answer: The Gers region generally offers pleasant cycling conditions during spring (April-May) and autumn (September-October) when temperatures are milder. Summer can be warm, but still enjoyable, while winter might be cooler and wetter.

faqs.question: Are dogs allowed on this cycling route?
faqs.answer: This is a public road cycling route, so dogs are generally permitted if they are safely secured in a bike trailer or basket. Always ensure your dog is comfortable and safe during the ride, and adhere to local regulations regarding pets in public spaces, especially in villages.

faqs.question: Are there any permits or entrance fees required to cycle this route?
faqs.answer: No, there are no specific permits or entrance fees required to cycle this route, as it primarily uses public roads connecting the villages of Cologne and Sarrant.

faqs.question: Does this route intersect with other well-known cycling paths?
faqs.answer: Yes, sections of this route overlap with several local trails, including "Le sentier des pigeonniers à Sarrant," "Circuit le Cachou," and "La croix blanche." These intersections offer opportunities to explore shorter segments of other paths if you wish.

faqs.question: Are there any cafes or restaurants along the route?
faqs.answer: While the route itself is relatively short, both the starting point, Cologne, and the destination, the Medieval village of Sarrant, are charming villages that offer options for refreshments and meals. It's advisable to plan your stops in these towns.

faqs.question: Is this a long-distance touring route?
faqs.answer: No, this 18.7 km route is not a widely recognized long-distance touring trail. It's best enjoyed as a scenic day trip or a segment within a broader exploration of the Gers region, which offers many cycling opportunities.
